title: Prototype delegation

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Prototype delegation; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Prototype delegation
sources.claim: Objects delegate missing property lookup through a prototype chain, and constructor prototype properties can be shared by instances.

sources.locator: MDN JavaScript Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Prototype delegation
sources.claim: Objects delegate missing property lookup through a prototype chain, and constructor prototype properties can be shared by instances.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Objects delegate missing property lookup through a prototype chain, and constructor prototype properties can be shared by instances.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: An instance method is found on the class prototype while instance data remains an own property. Your goal is to inspect and explain a short prototype chain and a shadowed property. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Object records and property access; Function declarations, expressions and arrows; Method calls and this. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Objects delegate missing property lookup through a prototype chain, and constructor prototype properties can be shared by instances. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Shadowing, mutation of shared prototypes, null prototypes, and pollution from untrusted keys require care. This draft therefore rejects: Every object contains a private copied version of all prototype methods.

includes: Trace own and inherited property lookup without treating prototypes as class copies.
Inspect and explain a short prototype chain and a shadowed property.
excludes: Shadowing, mutation of shared prototypes, null prototypes, and pollution from untrusted keys require care.

outcomes: Inspect and explain a short prototype chain and a shadowed property.

examples.title: Prototype delegation: concrete case
examples.situation: An instance method is found on the class prototype while instance data remains an own property.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: An instance method is found on the class prototype while instance data remains an own property.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Prototype methods are shared and found by delegated lookup, not copied into every instance.
examples.result: The result follows because Prototype methods are shared and found by delegated lookup, not copied into every instance.

examples.title: Prototype delegation: boundary case
examples.situation: Shadowing, mutation of shared prototypes, null prototypes, and pollution from untrusted keys require care.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Shadowing, mutation of shared prototypes, null prototypes, and pollution from untrusted keys require care.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Every object contains a private copied version of all prototype methods.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Prototype methods are shared and found by delegated lookup, not copied into every instance.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: Every object contains a private copied version of all prototype methods.

analogy.example: A coordinated event desk can model Prototype delegation, with values, tasks, callbacks, and external messages changing observable state.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Prototype delegation before using exact platform terms
limitations: Human coordination does not reproduce coercion, lexical scope, microtasks, prototypes, or browser security boundaries.

recap.summary: You should now be able to inspect and explain a short prototype chain and a shadowed property. Keep this boundary in view: Shadowing, mutation of shared prototypes, null prototypes, and pollution from untrusted keys require care.
